why_now: A real writer would cross from artifacts to persistent DB state.
mitigation_if_future: Require separate DDL, dry-run writer review, manual confirmation and test DB first.

why_now: Real DB target handling is not needed for current TFG deliverables.
mitigation_if_future: Environment-only, boolean audit, no printing, secret scan and review.

why_now: Runtime tables do not exist and DDL would be a separate schema decision.
mitigation_if_future: Separate DDL design, migration review, rollback/backup plan.

why_now: 54 preview_would_insert rows can be misread as runtime inserts if a real writer follows too soon.
mitigation_if_future: Keep labels explicit and rows_inserted=0 in memory/roadmap.

why_now: Runtime SQL rows could appear as active live monitoring rather than simulated ledger.
mitigation_if_future: UI copy and read-only badges before consuming runtime ledger.

risk: Telegram or AI interprets ledger as signal
why_now: Future consumers are pending and could overinterpret persisted events.
mitigation_if_future: Keep consumers read-only and block operational wording.

why_now: Append-only runtime tables need retention, pruning and query policy.
mitigation_if_future: Define retention and archival before DB writes.

why_now: Writer real adds a new DB state surface to test and support.
mitigation_if_future: Implement only when a consumer requires it.

why_now: A persisted order-intent-like ledger can look like an execution queue.
mitigation_if_future: Explicitly state not MT5 input and require MT5 gate later.

why_now: Real runtime writes might be presented as a live system in the memory.
mitigation_if_future: Frame SQL runtime as future work if not implemented.
